summary: The diff implements a substantial portion of the requested Pokemon RPG game, covering most requirements including the Professor Oak intro, starter selection, real-time battle system, story campaign, gym battles, Team Rocket encounters, evolution system, backend persistence, and anime visual theme. However, several specific acceptance criteria and features have gaps or are only partially implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Move collision detection resolves with correct win/lose/cancel outcomes based on power comparison when two opposing moves collide simultaneously
missing_requirements.reason: The Battle.tsx implements real-time move execution and dodge mechanics, but there is no evidence of projectile collision detection between opposing moves mid-flight. The system applies damage directly rather than detecting when a player move and opponent move physically collide and comparing their power values.

missing_requirements.requirement: Strategy input is stored per battle for replay/review in the backend
missing_requirements.reason: StrategyInput.tsx handles keyword parsing and triggers UI responses, but there is no evidence that strategy text is persisted to the backend per battle. The backend main.mo and useQueries.ts do not show a strategy storage endpoint.

missing_requirements.requirement: Final arc battle features dramatic cinematic sequences for Ash vs. the champion
missing_requirements.reason: A StoryCampaign page is referenced in App.tsx and routes exist, but the file was omitted from the diff. There is no evidence of a championship cinematic sequence being implemented.

missing_requirements.requirement: Gym battles are noticeably harder than regular wild/story battles
missing_requirements.reason: GymBattles.tsx routes the player to the standard /battle page with bgType=gym but uses the same OPPONENT_POKEMON pool offset by gym id. There is no evidence of gym-specific difficulty scaling, increased HP pools, or stronger move sets compared to wild encounters.

missing_requirements.requirement: Defeating Team Rocket plays a blasting off sound effect
missing_requirements.reason: TeamRocketEncounter.tsx implements a blast-off CSS animation visually, but there is no sound effect triggered on blast off. The requirement explicitly mentions a sound effect.

missing_requirements.requirement: Battle opponent pool contains at least 30 distinct Pokemon with unique move sets
missing_requirements.reason: pokemonData.ts is truncated and the full OPPONENT_POKEMON array count is not visible in the diff. The 30-Pokemon minimum cannot be confirmed.

missing_requirements.requirement: Attack animations use particle effects, flash, and motion blur to feel cinematic with dynamic camera angles
missing_requirements.reason: AttackAnimation.tsx uses emoji-based projectiles and a simple screen flash overlay. There is no React Three Fiber usage, genuine particle systems, or dynamic camera angle changes as requested in the build constraints.

missing_requirements.requirement: Battle history stores last 20 battles with opponent and outcome
missing_requirements.reason: useQueries.ts shows createBattleLog and getLog hooks, but the backend main.mo is truncated and does not clearly show a capped 20-battle history. The getLog return type is string[] which may not store structured opponent and outcome data.

hallucinated_features.description: Internet Identity (ICP) authentication login system with a dedicated LoginPage and blockchain-based actor calls
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not mention Internet Computer, ICP, Internet Identity, or any blockchain-based authentication. The diff includes a full InternetIdentityProvider, useInternetIdentity hook, LoginPage route, and actor-based backend calls that were never requested.

hallucinated_features.description: Caffeine.ai attribution footer link embedded in the Layout
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request does not request any third-party attribution or external promotional links. The Layout.tsx includes a hardcoded Caffeine.ai referral link in the footer.

hallucinated_features.description: Access control and admin permission system in the backend
hallucinated_features.reason: The build request makes no mention of an admin role, access control layers, or permission systems. The backend includes MixinAuthorization, AccessControl, and admin token initialization logic.
